mnemonics.title: Network Types by Scale
mnemonics.content: LAN → MAN → WAN: Think 'Local Metro Wide' - like a train going from Local station, to Metro station, then going Wide (across country)

mnemonics.title: Bus Topology Issues
mnemonics.content: BUS: Backbone Used, Single failure - when the backbone cable breaks, all systems shut down

mnemonics.title: Star Topology
mnemonics.content: STAR: Single Terminal At the hub causes a collapse - if the central hub fails, everything fails

mnemonics.title: Transmission Modes
mnemonics.content: S-H-F: Simple Half Full - Simplex is Simple one-way, Half can take turns, Full is Fun (both ways)

acronyms.usage: Office, home, school network - confined area
Secure remote access to private networks

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a house (LAN) → neighborhood (CAN) → city (MAN) → country/world (WAN) - like zooming out on a map
Think of a wheel with spokes - hub is the center, devices are spokes. Remove center, wheel collapses
Think of a relay race track - token passes like a baton from runner to runner
Imagine a fishing net - many interconnected holes; if one string breaks, other paths still exist

analogies.analogy: Like a single-lane road with houses on both sides. One accident blocks the whole road.
Like a hub-and-spoke airline system. All flights go through major hubs.
Like a circle of friends passing a message around - one break and message stops.
LAN is like your home's plumbing (within walls); WAN is like city's water supply system (across areas).
Like a restaurant: clients (customers) order from server (waiter) who gets food from kitchen (server).
Like a potluck dinner where everyone brings and shares food directly - no middle person.
